query: Design the automated test strategy for `scripts/analyze-skill-eval-results.py`. Produce the test plan.
expected_skill_contribution: The qa-automation agent should apply the test pyramid (70% unit / 20% integration / 10% E2E), define coverage gate ≥80%, and specify CI gates — baseline gives a generic test plan.
fixture_intent: Confirm that the agent structures the test plan around the pyramid ratio rather than listing tests ad hoc, and that coverage gate and CI blocking conditions are explicit and measurable rather than aspirational.
baseline_gaps: Produces a flat list of tests without structuring them into unit / integration / E2E layers with pyramid ratio targets
Mentions coverage as a goal without specifying a numeric gate (≥80% branches) that blocks merge
Describes CI usage generically without specifying which test levels gate merge and which run on other triggers
expected_behavior: Structures the test plan explicitly as: unit tests ~70% of total cases, integration tests ~20%, E2E tests ~10% — with example test cases for each layer
Specifies the coverage gate as ≥80% branch coverage required before merge, not as a recommendation but as a hard gate

Identifies at least 3 of the 8 mandatory edge cases to cover: empty input, malformed JSON, missing keys, boundary score values, multiple result files, zero-result inputs, encoding errors, and large file performance
Specifies CI gate precisely: unit + integration tests must pass before merge; E2E tests run on critical paths only; coverage report published as artifact

query: Run the test suite and quarantine any flaky tests found. Document the quarantine and root cause for each.
expected_skill_contribution: The qa-automation agent should detect flakiness (run 3-5x), quarantine with test.fixme(), identify root cause (race/animation/network), and file a Beads issue — not just re-run until it passes.
fixture_intent: Verify that the agent treats intermittent failure as a first-class signal requiring quarantine and root-cause analysis rather than as noise to be suppressed by re-running until green.
baseline_gaps: Re-runs failing tests repeatedly until they pass without marking them as flaky or applying any quarantine
Does not identify a root cause category for the intermittent failure — treats it as a one-off fluke
Omits filing a Beads issue for the quarantined test's root cause investigation
expected_behavior: Runs the test suite 3-5 times to detect intermittent failures — does not conclude flakiness from a single failure or a single pass
Quarantines confirmed flaky tests with `test.fixme(true, 'Flaky: Issue #N')` or language-equivalent marker that prevents false green CI results
Identifies root cause category for each quarantined test: race condition / animation timing / network timing — not a generic 'needs investigation' label
Produces a `bd create "Fix flaky test: <name>" -t task -p 2 --json` command for each quarantined test's root cause investigation, linking back to the quarantined test location

query: Build the complete CI test pipeline for the harness eval scripts: unit tests, integration tests, coverage gate, and test report artifacts.
expected_skill_contribution: The qa-automation agent should design CI pipeline with all three test levels, coverage gate blocking merge, JUnit XML output, and artifact upload — not just describe what tests to write.
fixture_intent: Stress-test the agent's CI pipeline design depth — the discriminant is whether it produces a concrete workflow spec (YAML or equivalent) with gates, artifact definitions, and flaky-test threshold, versus prose descriptions of what could be tested.
baseline_gaps: Describes which tests should exist without specifying a CI workflow YAML structure, job dependencies, or step ordering
Does not specify artifact upload for test reports or coverage HTML — leaves observability as an afterthought
Omits a numeric flaky-test threshold for pipeline health assessment
expected_behavior: Specifies CI gates as workflow conditions: unit + integration tests must pass; coverage ≥80% must pass; both conditions block merge via required status checks — not advisory
Specifies test report format: JUnit XML for test visibility in CI UI, coverage HTML uploaded as a workflow artifact with retention policy
Defines E2E scope explicitly: critical eval script workflows only (~10% of suite) — lists the specific workflows included, not just the percentage
Applies the flaky-test threshold: pipeline health requires <5% flaky test rate; exceeding threshold triggers automatic quarantine step in the workflow
